heading: Assessing and modeling named storms over coastal States
label: 3611 Assessing and modeling named storms over coastal States

children.heading: COASTAL Formula
children.label: 1 COASTAL Formula
children.text: The term “COASTAL Formula” has the meaning given the term in .section 4057(a) of title 42

children.heading: Coastal State
children.label: 2 Coastal State
children.text: The term “coastal State” has the meaning given the term “coastal state” in , except that the term shall not apply with respect to a State or territory that has an operational wind and flood loss allocation system.section 1453 of title 16

children.heading: Coastal waters
children.label: 3 Coastal waters
children.text: The term “coastal waters” has the meaning given the term in such section.

children.label: A
children.text: collected before, during, or after such storm; and

children.label: B
children.text: necessary to determine magnitude and timing of wind speeds, rainfall, the barometric pressure, river flows, the extent, height, and timing of storm surge, topographic and bathymetric data, and other measures required to accurately model and assess damage from such storm.

children.heading: Indeterminate loss
children.label: 5 Indeterminate loss
children.text: The term “indeterminate loss” has the meaning given the term in .section 4057(a) of title 42

children.heading: Named storm
children.label: 6 Named storm
children.text: The term “named storm” means any organized weather system with a defined surface circulation and maximum sustained winds of at least 39 miles per hour which the National Hurricane Center of the United States National Weather Service names as a tropical storm or a hurricane.

children.heading: Named Storm Event Model
children.label: 7 Named Storm Event Model
children.text: The term “Named Storm Event Model” means the official meteorological and oceanographic computerized model, developed by the Administrator under subsection (b)(1)(A), which utilizes covered data to replicate the magnitude, timing, and spatial variations of winds, rainfall, and storm surges associated with named storms for which post-storm assessments are conducted.

children.heading: Participant
children.label: 8 Participant
children.text: The term “participant” means a Federal, State, or private entity that chooses to cooperate with the Administrator in carrying out the provisions of this section by collecting, contributing, and maintaining covered data.

children.heading: Post-storm assessment
children.label: 9 Post-storm assessment
children.text: The term “post-storm assessment” means a scientific assessment produced and certified by the Administrator to determine the magnitude, timing, and spatial variations of winds, rainfall, and storm surges associated with a specific named storm to be used in the COASTAL Formula.

children.heading: State
children.label: 10 State
children.text: The term “State” means a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Named Storm Event Model and post-storm assessment
children.label: b Named Storm Event Model and post-storm assessment

children.heading: Establishment of Named Storm Event Model
children.label: 1 Establishment of Named Storm Event Model

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Administrator shall develop the Named Storm Event Model.December 31, 2020

children.heading: Accuracy
children.label: B Accuracy
children.text: The Named Storm Event Model shall be designed to generate post-storm assessments, as provided in paragraph (2), that have a degree of accuracy of not less than 90 percent for an indeterminate loss for which a post-storm assessment is utilized.

children.heading: Public review
children.label: C Public review
children.text: The Administrator shall seek input and suggestions from the public before the Named Storm Event Model, or any modification to the Named Storm Event Model, takes effect.

children.heading: Identification of named storms threatening coastal States
children.label: A Identification of named storms threatening coastal States
children.text: After the establishment of the COASTAL Formula, the Administrator shall, in consultation with the Secretary of Homeland Security, identify named storms that may reasonably constitute a threat to any portion of a coastal State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Upon identification of a named storm under subparagraph (A), and pursuant to the protocol established under subsection (c), the Administrator may deploy sensors to enhance the collection of covered data in the areas in coastal States that the Administrator determines are at the highest risk of experiencing geophysical events that would cause indeterminate losses.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: ii Rule of construction
children.text: If the Administrator takes action under clause (i), that action may not be construed as indicating that a post-storm assessment will be developed for any coastal State in which that action is taken.

children.heading: Identification of indeterminate losses in coastal States
children.label: C Identification of indeterminate losses in coastal States
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the first date on which sustained winds of not less than 39 miles per hour are measured in a coastal State during a named storm identified under subparagraph (A), the Secretary of Homeland Security shall notify the Administrator with respect to the existence of any indeterminate losses in that coastal State resulting from that named storm.

children.heading: Post-storm assessment required
children.label: D Post-storm assessment required
children.text: Upon confirmation of indeterminate losses identified under subparagraph (C) with respect to a named storm, the Administrator shall develop a post-storm assessment for each coastal State that suffered such indeterminate losses as a result of the named storm using the Named Storm Event Model and covered data collected for such named storm pursuant to the protocol established under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Submittal of post-storm assessment
children.label: E Submittal of post-storm assessment
children.text: Not later than 90 days after any indeterminate losses are identified under subparagraph (C), the Administrator shall submit to the Secretary of Homeland Security the post-storm assessment developed under subparagraph (D) for any coastal State that suffered such indeterminate losses.

children.heading: Separate post-storm assessments for a single named storm
children.label: F Separate post-storm assessments for a single named storm

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Administrator may conduct a separate post-storm assessment for each coastal State in which indeterminate losses are identified under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Timeline
children.label: ii Timeline
children.text: If the Administrator conducts a separate post-storm assessment under clause (i), the Administrator shall complete the assessment based on the dates of actions that the Administrator takes under subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Accuracy
children.label: 3 Accuracy
children.text: The Administrator shall ensure, to the greatest extent practicable, that each post-storm assessment developed under paragraph (2) has a degree of accuracy of not less than 90 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: certify the degree of accuracy for such assessment, including specific reference to any segments or geographic areas for which the assessment is less than 90 percent accurate; and

children.label: B
children.text: report such certification to the Secretary of Homeland Security for the purposes of use with indeterminate loss claims under .section 4057 of title 42

children.heading: Finality of determinations
children.label: 5 Finality of determinations
children.text: A certification of the degree of accuracy of a post-storm assessment under this subsection by the Administrator shall be final and shall not be subject to judicial review.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 6 Availability
children.text: The Administrator shall make available to the public the Named Storm Event Model and any post-storm assessment developed under this subsection.

children.heading: Establishment of a protocol for post-storm assessment
children.label: c Establishment of a protocol for post-storm assessment

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , the Administrator shall establish a protocol, based on the plan submitted under subsection (d)(3), to collect and assemble all covered data required by the Administrator to produce post-storm assessments required by subsection (b), including assembling data collected by participants and stored in the database established under subsection (f) and from such other sources as the Administrator considers appropriate.December 31, 2020

children.heading: Acquisition of sensors and structures
children.label: 2 Acquisition of sensors and structures
children.text: If the Administrator is unable to use a public or private asset to obtain covered data as part of the protocol established under paragraph (1), the Administrator may acquire such sensors and structures for the placement of sensors as may, in the discretion of the Administrator, be necessary to obtain such data.

children.heading: Use of Federal assets
children.label: 3 Use of Federal assets
children.text: If the protocol requires placement of a sensor to develop assessments pursuant to subsection (b), the Administrator shall, to the extent practicable, use Federal assets for the placement of such sensors.

children.label: i
children.text: meteorological data;

children.label: ii
children.text: national security-related data;

children.label: iii
children.text: navigation-related data;

children.label: iv
children.text: hydrographic data; or

children.label: v
children.text: such other data as the Administrator considers appropriate.

children.heading: Receipt of consideration
children.label: B Receipt of consideration
children.text: The Administrator may receive and expend consideration for the placement of a sensor on a structure under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In-kind consideration
children.label: C In-kind consideration
children.text: Consideration received under subparagraph (B) may be received in-kind.

children.heading: Use of consideration
children.label: D Use of consideration
children.text: To the extent practicable, consideration received under subparagraph (B) shall be used for the maintenance of sensors used to collect covered data.

children.heading: Coordinated deployments and data collection practices
children.label: 5 Coordinated deployments and data collection practices
children.text: The Administrator shall, in consultation with the Office of the Federal Coordinator for Meteorology, coordinate the deployment of sensors as part of the protocol established under paragraph (1) and related data collection carried out by Federal, State, academic, and private entities who choose to cooperate with the Administrator in carrying out this subsection.

children.heading: Priority acquisition and deployment
children.label: 6 Priority acquisition and deployment
children.text: The Administrator shall give priority in the acquisition for and deployment of sensors under the protocol required by paragraph (1) to areas of coastal States that have the highest risk of being harmed by named storms.

children.heading: Assessment of systems and efforts to collect covered data
children.label: d Assessment of systems and efforts to collect covered data

children.heading: Identification of systems and efforts to collect covered data
children.label: 1 Identification of systems and efforts to collect covered data
A
children.text: carry out a survey to identify all Federal and State efforts and systems that are capable of collecting covered data; and

children.label: B
children.text: consult with private and academic sector entities to identify domestic private and academic systems that are capable of collecting covered data.

children.heading: Identification of gaps
children.label: 2 Identification of gaps
children.text: The Administrator shall, in consultation with the Office of the Federal Coordinator for Meteorology and individuals and entities consulted under subsection (e)(3), assess the systems identified under paragraph (1) and identify which systems meet the needs of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration for the collection of covered data, including with respect to the accuracy requirement for post-storm assessment under subsection (b)(3).

children.heading: Plan
children.label: 3 Plan
children.text: Not later than 270 days after , the Administrator shall, in consultation with the Office of the Federal Coordinator for Meteorology, submit to Congress a plan for the collection of covered data necessary to develop the Named Storm Event Model and post-storm assessment required by subsection (b) that addresses any gaps identified in paragraph (2).July 6, 2012

children.heading: Coordination of covered data collection and maintenance by participants
children.label: e Coordination of covered data collection and maintenance by participants
A
children.text: to streamline the process of collecting covered data in accordance with the protocol established under subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: to maintain transparency of such process and the database established under subsection (f).

children.label: A
children.text: academic research;

children.label: B
children.text: private sector use;

children.label: C
children.text: public outreach; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other purposes as the Administrator considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: The Commanding General of the Corps of Engineers.

children.label: B
children.text: The Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.label: C
children.text: The Commandant of the Coast Guard.

children.label: D
children.text: The Director of the United States Geological Survey.

children.label: E
children.text: The Office of the Federal Coordinator for Meteorology.

children.label: F
children.text: The Director of the National Science Foundation.

children.label: G
children.text: The Administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

children.label: H
children.text: Such public, private, and academic sector entities as the Administrator considers appropriate for purposes of carrying out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Establishment of Coastal Wind and Water Event Database
children.label: f Establishment of Coastal Wind and Water Event Database
A
children.text: to support the protocol established under subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: for the purposes listed in subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: Designation
children.label: 2 Designation
children.text: The database established under paragraph (1) shall be known as the “Coastal Wind and Water Event Database”.

children.label: A
children.text: examine duplicated Federal efforts to collect covered data; and

children.label: B
children.text: determine the cost effectiveness of such efforts; and

submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives a report on the findings of the Comptroller General with respect to the audit completed under paragraph (1).1So in original. Probably should be followed by “Committee on”.1
